turns.content: If the company's revenue grew 12% YoY but costs grew 18%, what does that imply about margins?
Good question. If costs outpace revenue growth, margins are being squeezed â€” profitability is declining even as the top line grows.
Right, so if margins were 20% last year and revenue was $100M, what would net income be this year roughly?
